commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=1f00685e570d5918046ad64ff62a8bab4a5f3a3e)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds two documented ACP turn internal hook events, manager and dispatch plumbing, type guards, and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +210, Tests +551, Docs +9. Total +770 across 17 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the blocking patch issue by source inspection: PR head skips `agent:turn:save` when no save callback is supplied, while the direct ACP agent-command path omits that callback and persists its transcript afterward. I did not run tests because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the remaining blockers are API/session-state contract approval and contributor real-behavior proof, not a safe automated repair lane.

target.title: feat(hooks): add agent:turn:end and agent:turn:save internal hook events
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: ACP turn completion updates latency/error stats, but there was no internal hook surface that distinguished "the runtime turn ended" from "the turn save phase reached an outcome."
- Why it matters: Existing signals are per-message, sub-turn, or session-level. Post-turn automation such as memory, indexing, audit, and analytics need a deterministic turn boundary, and some consumers specifically need to know whether durable session state is readable.
- What changed: Added two narrow ACP turn internal hook events:
  - `agent:turn:end`: lifecycle boundary after ACP terminal event delivery, emitted on success and failure.
  - `agent:turn:save`: persistence outcome boundary after the ACP turn save phase resolves; its `success` field describes save success/failure and `turnSuccess` preserves the runtime turn outcome.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This PR does not add `agent:turn:start`, new config, user-facing messaging changes, `CHANGELOG.md` edits, or broader lifecycle refactors.
- Review context: This keeps the #37833 lineage and implements ClawSweeper's "split timing semantics before merge" option without making a truthy event name carry failure semantics.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #37833
- Related #60734
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: N/A
- Missing detection / guardrail: N/A
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Internal hook consumers can subscribe to two ACP turn lifecycle events:

| Event | Fires when | Durable session-state guarantee |
|---|---|---|
| `agent:turn:end` | The ACP runtime turn completes or fails after terminal event delivery | No |
| `agent:turn:save` | The ACP turn save phase reaches an outcome after the dispatch transcript save step runs or declines | Only when `context.success === true` |

`agent:turn:end` carries `sessionKey`, `success`, `durationMs`, and optional `errorCode` for runtime turn failures.

`agent:turn:save` carries `sessionKey`, `success` for the save outcome, `turnSuccess` for the underlying runtime turn outcome, `durationMs`, optional `turnErrorCode`, and optional `saveError` when the save callback throws.

If saving the mirrored dispatch turn fails, `agent:turn:end` still fires and `agent:turn:save` fires with `success: false`. Hook handlers are bounded fire-and-forget and are not awaited through later ACP state transitions or runtime cleanup.

No user-facing messaging or config behavior changes.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[ACP turn completes] -> [latency/error stats only]

After:
[ACP turn completes]
  -> [latency/error stats]
  -> [agent:turn:end internal hook]
  -> [save mirrored ACP dispatch turn]
  -> [agent:turn:save internal hook]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): ACP manager with an in-process test runtime backend
- Relevant config (redacted): isolated test config/state

### Steps

1. Register internal hook listeners for `agent:turn:end` and `agent:turn:save`.
2. Trigger a successful ACP turn through `AcpSessionManager`.
3. Trigger a failing ACP turn through `AcpSessionManager`.
4. Trigger a successful ACP turn whose save step fails.
5. Trigger ACP dispatch and verify transcript persistence runs before successful `agent:turn:save`.

### Expected

- Successful saves emit `agent:turn:end` and `agent:turn:save` with `success: true` on both events.
- Failed turns emit `agent:turn:end` with `success: false` and `errorCode`.
- Failed turns with an ACP dispatch save callback emit `agent:turn:save` with `success: false`, `turnSuccess: false`, and `turnErrorCode`.
- Save failures do not suppress `agent:turn:end`; `agent:turn:save` fires with `success: false` and `saveError`.
- Dispatch-backed ACP turns persist transcript output before successful `agent:turn:save` can report `success: true`.
- Hook listener failures do not break the turn lifecycle.

### Actual

- Added manager-level tests covering successful, failed, saved, save-ineligible, and save-failure turns.
- Added ACP dispatch tests covering transcript persistence before successful `agent:turn:save` and persistence failure being reported as a failed save outcome.
- Added internal-hook type guard coverage for both hook events.
- Updated hook docs to document the split timing contract.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: ACP turn hooks now expose two distinct internal boundaries: runtime turn completion via `agent:turn:end` and persistence outcome via `agent:turn:save`.
- **Real environment tested**: macOS local OpenClaw source checkout at `1f00685e57`, running production `AcpSessionManager` and the production internal hook registry with an in-process ACP runtime backend.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `PROOF_HEAD=$(git rev-parse --short=10 HEAD) pnpm exec tsx ./tmp-pr-71399-hook-proof.ts`. The temporary proof harness registered real internal hook listeners, drove `AcpSessionManager.runTurn()` through save success, runtime failure with save declined, save callback failure, and save-declined paths, then asserted the emitted hooks.
- **Evidence after fix**: terminal console output from the local proof run:

```text
PR #71399 hook proof
head: 1f00685e57
environment: local OpenClaw source checkout; production AcpSessionManager and internal hook registry; in-process ACP runtime backend

case: success-save
  rejectedCode: none
  order: event:text_delta -> event:done -> save:ok -> hook:agent:turn:end -> hook:agent:turn:save
  hooks: agent:turn:end{success=true, turnSuccess=none, durationMs=number, errorCode=none, turnErrorCode=none, saveError=none}, agent:turn:save{success=true, turnSuccess=true, durationMs=number, errorCode=none, turnErrorCode=none, saveError=none}

case: runtime-failure-save-declined
  rejectedCode: ACP_TURN_FAILED
  order: event:error -> save:false -> hook:agent:turn:end -> hook:agent:turn:save
  hooks: agent:turn:end{success=false, turnSuccess=none, durationMs=number, errorCode=ACP_TURN_FAILED, turnErrorCode=none, saveError=none}, agent:turn:save{success=false, turnSuccess=false, durationMs=number, errorCode=none, turnErrorCode=ACP_TURN_FAILED, saveError=none}

case: save-failure
  rejectedCode: none
  order: event:done -> save:throw -> hook:agent:turn:end -> hook:agent:turn:save
  hooks: agent:turn:end{success=true, turnSuccess=none, durationMs=number, errorCode=none, turnErrorCode=none, saveError=none}, agent:turn:save{success=false, turnSuccess=true, durationMs=number, errorCode=none, turnErrorCode=none, saveError=durable transcript write failed}

case: save-returned-false
  rejectedCode: none
  order: event:done -> save:false -> hook:agent:turn:end -> hook:agent:turn:save
  hooks: agent:turn:end{success=true, turnSuccess=none, durationMs=number, errorCode=none, turnErrorCode=none, saveError=none}, agent:turn:save{success=false, turnSuccess=true, durationMs=number, errorCode=none, turnErrorCode=none, saveError=none}

assertions: passed
```

- **Observed result after fix**: runtime success with durable save produced both hooks with successful outcomes; runtime failure produced `agent:turn:end` with `success=false` and `errorCode=ACP_TURN_FAILED` plus `agent:turn:save` with `success=false`, `turnSuccess=false`, and `turnErrorCode=ACP_TURN_FAILED`; save callback failure produced `agent:turn:save` with `success=false`, `turnSuccess=true`, and `saveError`.
- **What was not tested**: a networked external `acpx` adapter process; this proof exercises OpenClaw's ACP manager and internal hook registry with an in-process runtime backend.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - `agent:turn:end` type guard recognizes the event shape, including optional `errorCode`
  - `agent:turn:save` type guard recognizes the event shape, including `turnSuccess`, optional `turnErrorCode`, and optional `saveError`
  - ACP manager emits `agent:turn:end` after successful turns
  - ACP manager emits `agent:turn:save` after successful save turns
  - ACP manager emits `agent:turn:end` after failed turns with `errorCode: "ACP_TURN_FAILED"`
  - ACP manager emits `agent:turn:save` when the save callback fails, carrying `success: false` and `saveError`
  - ACP manager emits `agent:turn:save` when the save callback returns `false`, carrying `success: false`
  - terminal ACP events are delivered before `agent:turn:end`
  - ACP dispatch persists transcript output before successful `agent:turn:save`
  - hook dispatch uses `fireAndForgetBoundedHook(() => triggerInternalHook(...))`
  - docs describe the separate lifecycle and durable-state semantics
- Commands run locally for this revision:
  - `pnpm test src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/automation/hooks.md src/acp/control-plane/manager.core.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.types.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts`
  - `pnpm lint:core`
  - `pnpm check:test-types`
  - `git diff --check`
- What I did **not** verify:
  - a networked external `acpx` adapter process; this PR verifies the OpenClaw ACP manager, dispatch, and internal-hook seams
  - full `pnpm check:changed`; the local Testbox wrapper failed before execution because `crabbox` did not pass its own sanity check

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery

- Revert this PR to remove the new internal hook events and return ACP turn completion to stats-only behavior.
- Existing config and user-facing messaging do not need migration or rollback steps.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Hook consumers may need either runtime lifecycle completion or durable saved-state readiness, and those are different timing contracts.
  - Mitigation: Split the surface into `agent:turn:end` for lifecycle completion and `agent:turn:save` for persistence outcome reporting, with docs and tests for both.
- Risk: Slow hook handlers could delay turn completion if internal hook execution were awaited.
  - Mitigation: Hook dispatch remains bounded/fire-and-forget via `fireAndForgetBoundedHook()`.
target.author: mantisai-bot
